parts.instruction: Questions 11–12 and 13–14: Choose TWO A–E. Questions 15–20: Label the map A–I.

questions.prompt: What are the TWO main reasons why this site has been chosen for the housing development?

options.label: There is easy access to local facilities.
It has good connections with the airport.
The land is of little agricultural value.
It will be convenient for workers.

the low cost of all the accommodation

parts.instruction: Questions 21–24: Choose A, B or C. Questions 25–26: Choose TWO A–E. Questions 27–30: Matching A–F.

questions.prompt: Why do the students think the Laki eruption of 1783 is so important?

options.label: It was the most severe eruption in modern times.
It led to the formal study of volcanoes.
It had a profound effect on society.

questions.sectionInstruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.prompt: What surprised Adam about observations made at the time?

options.label: the number of places producing them
the lack of scientific data to support them

questions.prompt: According to Michelle, what did the contemporary sources say about the Laki haze?

options.label: People thought it was similar to ordinary fog.
It was associated with health issues.
It completely blocked out the sun for weeks.

questions.prompt: Adam corrects Michelle when she claims that Benjamin Franklin

options.label: came to the wrong conclusion about the cause of the haze.
was the first to identify the reason for the haze.
supported the opinions of other observers about the haze.

questions.prompt: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?

options.label: the number of deaths it caused
the speed at which the volcanic ash cloud spread
how people ignored the warning signs

questions.prompt: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?

options.label: the number of deaths it caused
the speed at which the volcanic ash cloud spread
how people ignored the warning signs

questions.prompt: Iceland

options.label: This country suffered the most severe loss of life.
The impact on agriculture was predictable.
There was a significant increase in deaths of young people.
This country saw the highest rise in food prices in the world.
It caused a particularly harsh winter.
